name: grace_prevents_sensor_repause
description: After user resumes from door/window pause, 90-min grace blocks immediate re-pause when same sensor opens again

events.note: Hot outdoor — nat-vent not viable (above threshold)
Door opens again 1 minute after resume, well within the 90-min grace window — grace should block re-pause.

assertions.reason: Sensor closes, automation resumes from pause
If door opens again within the 90-min grace window, automation should NOT re-pause — outcome stays the carried-forward 'resumed' from 14:45 (no new pause decision). Corrected from legacy 'no_action'.

verdict.summary: Grace period prevents immediate re-pause after user resumes from door/window pause
verdict.observed_behavior: Door opens within grace → automation ignores sensor for grace duration
verdict.expected_behavior: 90-min grace prevents sensor chatter from causing repeated pause/resume cycles after user intervenes

notes: Issue #476: migrated to use_coordinator=true, skip_startup_coalesce=true, evaluated for real. The scenario was missing the actual sensor re-open event the 14:46 assertion refers to — added it. automation_grace_seconds set explicitly to 5400 (90 min) to match the scenario's own stated intent ('90-min grace' in the description) — the harness default (automation_grace_seconds=300/5min, which is what the sensor-closed-resume path actually reads, not manual_grace_seconds) would have made the '90-min' claim inaccurate, though the specific 14:45->14:46 (1 min) assertion timing would have passed with either duration.

The 14:46 assertion was 'no_action' in the legacy-simulator version. outcomes.py's production_outcome_at() has no 'no_action' sentinel — corrected to 'resumed' (carried forward from 14:45), the correct way to assert 'no NEW decision fired' — same pattern as grace_full_lifecycle_sensor_still_open.json's 13:12 assertion.

This scenario exercises the same grace-blocks-reopen guard (automation.py:2137-2150) already revert-tested with fresh evidence in grace_full_lifecycle_sensor_still_open.json — not re-run here to avoid redundant proof of the same guard.
